name: Giribaile Reservoir Circular Route via the Ariza Bridge
name_original: Embalse de Giribaile — circular por el Puente Ariza
description: Jog this 6.1-mile (9.8 km) moderate route around Embalse de Giribaile, enjoying views of the reservoir and the historic Puente Ariza.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Embalse del Giribaile are set within the Guadalquivir Basin, where the Guadalimar River feeds an expansive artificial lake. The region is characterized by gentle hills adorned with olive groves, providing a picturesque backdrop for running. This area offers a blend of natural beauty and historical interest, making it suitable for outdoor activities. The reservoir itself is a significant feature, influencing the landscape and offering scenic views. Best jogging routes around Embalse del Giribaile The most popular jogging route is Giribaile Reservoir Circular Route via the Ariza Bridge, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 1 minute to complete. This route offers scenic views of the water and the historic Puente Ariza. Another top favourite among local runners is Castle and archaeological site of Giribaile, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This route provides views of the historic castle and archaeological features. Local runners also love the Giribaile Mines — round trip with views of the Giribaile Castle, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the Giribaile Castle, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. Jogging around Embalse del Giribaile is defined by reservoir views, gentle hills, and olive groves. The network offers options for moderate ability levels, with routes showcasing both natural landscapes and historical sites. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Embalse del Giribaile's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available around Embalse del Giribaile?
A: There are &lt;em&gt;9 moderate running routes&lt;/em&gt; around Embalse del Giribaile, offering a variety of experiences for those looking to explore the area on foot.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Embalse del Giribaile?
A: Yes, the region is well-suited for circular runs. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364603929/giribaile-reservoir-circular-route-via-the-ariza-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Reservoir Circular Route via the Ariza Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.8 km long and offers scenic views of the water.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging routes?
A: The jogging routes around Embalse del Giribaile offer picturesque views of the expansive reservoir, gentle hills, and vast olive groves. You'll experience a blend of natural beauty and historical interest, with some routes providing glimpses of ancient structures.

Q: Are there any historical sights or landmarks to see while running?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in history. You can explore routes that pass by historical features, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989779016/castle-and-archaeological-site-of-giribaile" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and archaeological site of Giribaile&lt;/a&gt;. The submerged 16th-century Puente Ariza is also a unique attraction, occasionally visible depending on water levels.

Q: Are the running routes suitable for beginners?
A: All 9 routes around Embalse del Giribaile are classified as &lt;em&gt;moderate&lt;/em&gt;. While they are not specifically designated as 'easy' or 'beginner' routes, their moderate difficulty means they are generally accessible for individuals with a reasonable level of fitness.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting the Embalse del Giribaile for a run?
A: The Embalse del Giribaile area is equipped with parking facilities, making it convenient for visitors. There is a dedicated parking area available, particularly near the multi-purpose building and boat estacionamiento area.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the running trails?
A: Yes, there is a multi-purpose building with panoramic views of the reservoir. This facility includes a cafeteria-bar, a shop, an information point, and changing rooms, providing convenient amenities for runners and visitors.

Q: What is the typical length of a running trail in this region?
A: The running trails around Embalse del Giribaile vary in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364603929/giribaile-reservoir-circular-route-via-the-ariza-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Reservoir Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is 9.8 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989779016/castle-and-archaeological-site-of-giribaile" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and archaeological site of Giribaile&lt;/a&gt; trail is 7.8 km. You'll find options ranging from approximately 7 km to over 11 km.

Q: Can I combine my run with other outdoor activities at Embalse del Giribaile?
A: Absolutely. Beyond running, Embalse del Giribaile is popular for various outdoor pursuits. You can enjoy fishing, bathing, and general water tourism, with a dedicated jetty and a floating pontoon available for access to the water.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Giribaile Castle?
A: Yes, several routes provide views of the historic Giribaile Castle. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364550889/giribaile-mines-round-trip-with-views-of-the-giribaile-castle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Mines — round trip with views of the Giribaile Castle&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.4 km trail that specifically highlights these views.

Q: What makes the Embalse del Giribaile a unique place for running?
A: Its uniqueness comes from being the second largest artificial lake in Jaén, set amidst olive groves and gentle hills. The combination of expansive water views, historical elements like the submerged Puente Ariza, and well-maintained infrastructure for visitors creates a distinctive and appealing environment for outdoor activities like running.

Q: Are there any longer running options for more experienced runners?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo De Canena – Caño Fountain of Canena loop from Canena&lt;/a&gt; offers an 11.39 km route. While all routes are moderate, this one provides a greater distance to cover.
tips: Old bridge that helps to cross this narrower part of what is called the Giribaile Reservoir, close to the town of Úbeda and which takes the waters of the Río Guadalimar.
Reservoir that collects the waters of the Río Guadalimar and that extends over a good number of hectares. There are three other reservoirs in this area.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Embalse de Giribaile circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly made up of paved tracks, making it well-suited for jogging. While the elevation changes are not extreme, there are some undulating sections, so it's not a completely flat path.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is graded as 'moderate' and requires 'good fitness' due to its length of approximately 9.8 kilometers and some undulating sections. While not extremely challenging, beginners might find it a good goal to work towards.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: For jogging, most people can expect to complete the approximately 9.8-kilometer (6.1-mile) circular route in about 1 to 1.5 hours, depending on their pace and fitness level.

faqs.question: What are the main sights along the Embalse de Giribaile circular route?
faqs.answer: Joggers can enjoy picturesque views of the expansive Giribaile Reservoir, often called the 'Sea of La Loma'. A significant highlight is the monumental 16th-century Ariza Bridge, which becomes visible when water levels are low.

faqs.question: When is the best time to see the historic Puente Ariza?
faqs.answer: The Puente Ariza is usually submerged under the reservoir's waters. It becomes visible, sometimes in its full splendor, when water levels are low, particularly during dry seasons. Checking local water level reports before your visit might increase your chances of seeing it.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the route is circular around the Embalse de Giribaile. Look for designated parking near the reservoir or the general vicinity of the Ariza Bridge to begin your jog.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog restrictions for this particular route is not available. However, as a general rule for outdoor trails in Spain, dogs are often permitted but should be kept on a leash, especially around wildlife or other visitors. Please observe local signage.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to jog the Embalse de Giribaile circular route by the Puente Ariza. Access to the reservoir and its surrounding areas is generally open.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route would likely be during the spring (March-May) or autumn (September-November) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot in Jaén province, making midday jogging less comfortable. Winter offers cooler temperatures but might coincide with higher water levels, potentially obscuring the Puente Ariza.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Giribaile circular route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and length of nearly 10 kilometers, the full jogging route might be challenging for very young children. However, families with older children who are accustomed to longer walks or jogs could enjoy it. The paved surfaces make it accessible, and the scenic views of the reservoir and the historic Ariza Bridge offer points of interest.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is primarily an outdoor jogging route, and specific facilities like restrooms or dedicated water sources directly on the path are not mentioned. It's advisable to bring your own water and use facilities before starting your activity. The reservoir area does include a tourist pier, which might have some amenities.
